verdict_reasoning: EREG encodes epiregulin, an EGF-family ligand that is synthesized as a type-I single-pass transmembrane precursor (proepiregulin) anchored at the plasma membrane. This TM-anchored precursor mediates juxtacrine signaling before being cleaved by ADAM proteases (ADAM10/ADAM17) to release the soluble ectodomain. The membrane-anchored proform is real and surface-accessible, but it is transient — the dominant biological actor is the shed soluble growth factor. This fits the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern, making the call contextual/dual_localization rather than yes.
